title: Your Body Has a Second Skeleton Nobody Taught You
script: This sounds fake, but you have a completely separate internal framework — and doctors barely mention it. Your fascia — a web of connective tissue wrapping every muscle, organ, and nerve — forms a continuous full-body network with its own tension system. Cut one strand and the pull shifts across your entire body within seconds. But here's the wild part: fascia has more sensory nerve endings than muscle. It feels pain, pressure, and movement independently. That shoulder ache you blamed on posture? Your fascia moved the problem there from somewhere else entirely. And that's how.
search_summary: Fascia connective tissue forms a full-body tension network with more nerve endings than muscle.
comment_question: Is your chronic pain actually coming from somewhere completely different? 🤔
source_note: Journal of Bodywork and Movement Therapies, 2012
